properties.titulo_evento: US strikes Iranian sites after Iran launches drones, in latest Gulf flare-up
properties.descricao: U.S. forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites on Saturday after shooting down drones launched by Iran toward the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. military said.

properties.titulo_evento: Israel and Iran exchange attacks as ceasefire falters
properties.descricao: In effort to contain escalation, US President Donald Trump spoke with Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu, US media reported.

properties.titulo_evento: How Lebanon and Iran’s war of words became backdrop for latest Israel war
properties.descricao: Iran had warned that talks could be stopped and attacks would resume if Israel continued to hit Lebanon.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war day 101: Tensions escalate as Iran and Israel trade air attacks
properties.descricao: Tensions have escalated between Iran and Israel while ongoing diplomatic efforts have failed to yield lasting peace.

properties.titulo_evento: Missiles intercepted over occupied East Jerusalem and West Bank
properties.descricao: The Israeli army said it intercepted missiles launched from Iran on Monday in response to attacks from Israel.

properties.titulo_evento: Yamal, Williams to be available for Spain’s opening World Cup match
properties.descricao: Star forward Yamal expected to be ready after suffering a groin injury that kept him out of a pretournament friendly.

properties.titulo_evento: Satellite images show destruction of the US-Israel war on Iran
properties.descricao: From Iranian naval ports to US military bases across the Gulf, Al Jazeera shows 15 sites before and after the attacks.

properties.titulo_evento: Debris reported in Jordan after Iranian missiles aimed at Israel intercepte
properties.descricao: Missiles believed fired from Iran toward Israel were seen over Jordan, with one reportedly intercepted.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran and Israel trade threats after Tehran launches missiles
properties.descricao: Iran and Israel exchanged threats after Tehran launched missiles towards Israel in response to Israeli strikes on Beirut

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war live: Missiles fired at Israel after attacks as new war fears rise
properties.descricao: Escalation comes after Israel attacked Lebanon's capital, Beirut, killing at least two people and wounding 20.

properties.titulo_evento: North Korea’s Kim Jong Un inspects munitions at weapons factory
properties.descricao: North Korea has released photos of Kim Jong Un inspecting huge munitions at a weapons factory

properties.titulo_evento: Iran’s attack on Israel aims to restore deterrence but avoid return to war
properties.descricao: Iran's attack on Israel aims to restore deterrence but avoid return to war.

properties.titulo_evento: Videos show missiles launched from Iran into Israel
properties.descricao: Iranian media has released video showing missiles being launched towards Israel.

properties.titulo_evento: Celebrations seen in Tehran as missiles fly overhead towards Israel
properties.descricao: Iranian state TV broadcast scenes of celebration in Tehran as missiles flew overhead en route to Israel.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran fires missiles at Israel after Beirut attack ‘crossed all red lines’
properties.descricao: Iran's IRGC says the barrage of missiles is 'a warning' to Israel amid its ongoing siege of Lebanon.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran fires missiles at Israel after it attacked Beirut
properties.descricao: Videos show missiles over Israel as the Israeli military says Iran launched a new wave of attacks.

properties.titulo_evento: Zelensky's close European allies set out five conditions for peace talks
properties.descricao: The Ukrainian president's meeting with his staunchest European allies came as US President Donald Trump's focus shifts to the war with Iran.

properties.titulo_evento: Tech stocks plunge in Asia after record rally and renewed Middle East attacks
properties.descricao: Markets in South Korea and Japan are hit by a tech sell-off, and oil rises as Iran and Israel launch attacks at each other

properties.titulo_evento: Friendship or leverage: Why is Xi Jinping in North Korea?
properties.descricao: Beijing is trying to reassert influence over a strategically vital yet deeply unpredictable partner.

properties.titulo_evento: Steve Rosenberg: Russia's economic forum overshadowed by drone attacks on St Petersburg
properties.descricao: The BBC's Russia editor saw Putin's flagship economic event overshadowed by Ukrainian drones attacks.

properties.titulo_evento: Counting is underway in Armenia’s pivotal vote for the South Caucasus future
properties.descricao: Polls close in Armenian’s decisive election for the South Caucasus country and the region’s future, against the backdrop of a Russia-West clash

properties.titulo_evento: Man opens fire in central Israel killing one person and injuring five others
properties.descricao: Israeli media say the attacker carried out the shootings from inside his car, allowing him to reach several locations within a matter of minutes.

properties.titulo_evento: Washington could tap into Iran's frozen billions to pay for Gulf attack damage
properties.descricao: The Iranian assets Washington is considering reallocating could include frozen funds, along with ships previously seized by the United States.

properties.titulo_evento: Armenia goes to polls in pivotal vote to decide South Caucasus future
properties.descricao: Armenians will decide their future on Sunday against the backdrop of a Russia-West clash, as both the EU and the US support PM Pashinyan’s cautious pro-Western drive, while Russia embarked on an onslaught of economic and political pressures on Yereva...

properties.titulo_evento: China's Xi begins rare North Korea visit
properties.descricao: China's President Xi Jinping has kicked off his first visit to North Korea in seven years. It is also Xi's first international trip this year after hosting key summits with US and Russian leaders.

properties.titulo_evento: Middle East updates: Israel and Iran trade fresh strikes
properties.descricao: Iran and Israel have exchanged missile fire in the most serious confrontation since a ceasefire agreed in April. Meanwhile, Yemen's Houthi rebels have threatened to target ships in the Red Sea. Follow DW for the latest.

properties.titulo_evento: Reports: Iran uses war as cover for increased executions
properties.descricao: Iran already had one of the highest rates of capital punishment in the world. Now, rights groups say, the country is using the current conflict to carry out even more executions, in an attempt to repress dissent.

properties.titulo_evento: Pope Leo in Spain: More than a million line Madrid streets
properties.descricao: Huge crowds welcomed Pope Leo XIV to Madrid on Sunday. The pontiff is on a five-day trip around Spain during which he is meeting vulnerable people such as migrants and the homeless, and promoting "genuine human values."

properties.titulo_evento: Peru votes for new president — for ninth time in 10 years
properties.descricao: Peru could soon have its ninth president in 10 years, as voters choose between Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sanchez in a runoff election overshadowed by instability and crime.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany news: Munich airport briefly suspends flights due to smell of smoke
properties.descricao: Flight operations have resumed at Munich Airport after a brief suspension. Meanwhile, Chancellor Merz is headed to London to discuss the Ukraine war as Putin rejects face-to-face talks.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran closes airspace after 'warning' attacks against Israel
properties.descricao: Iran has closed part of its airspace after firing missiles toward Israel. Iran's Revolutionary Guard said earlier strikes were a "warning" after Israel struck the Lebanese capital of Beirut.

properties.titulo_evento: Armenia, once Russia's reliable ally, considers an EU future
properties.descricao: Armenians are set to vote on the country's future geopolitical direction on June 7. The election will be a test of public opinion in the long-standing Russia ally, where the population is increasingly eyeing the EU.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany beat USA before World Cup but injuries cast shadow
properties.descricao: Germany’s final match before their World Cup start ended with a 2-1 win. But a tournament-ending injury to rising star Lennart Karl and doubts about veteran Manuel Neuer mean coach Julian Nagelsmann has work to do.

properties.titulo_evento: Hunger in Oleshky: Ukraine asks for evacuation
properties.descricao: The Ukrainian city of Oleshky has been occupied by Russian troops since 2022. Many residents are cut off from the outside world and living in dire conditions. Now, Kyiv wants to rescue them.

properties.titulo_evento: How Donald Trump helped make Spain’s prime minister a ‘rockstar’
properties.descricao: Pedro Sánchez may be embattled at home, but his opposition to the war in Iran has boosted his clout on the rest of the continent.

properties.titulo_evento: Albania’s ‘flamingo revolution’ comes to Brussels
properties.descricao: The EU is struggling to land a new deal on consumer protections for airline passengers. After a week of intense negotiations that ultimately failed, transport ministers are meeting today to discuss the current state of play. The main sticking points:...

properties.titulo_evento: Oil Surges After Hostilities Between Iran and Israel Escalate
properties.descricao: Oil prices jumped and stocks plunged after Iran and Israel exchanged strikes, raising doubts about the future of fragile cease-fire in the Middle East.

properties.titulo_evento: OPEC Plus to Boost Oil Production as Ceasefire in Iran Remains Elusive
properties.descricao: The cartel’s move to increase output by 188,000 barrels per day is largely symbolic, with vast amounts of the world’s oil stranded by the effective shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz.

properties.titulo_evento: Alan Riding, Times Correspondent in Latin America and Paris, Dies at 82
properties.descricao: He was a cosmopolitan observer and interpreter of societies he knew firsthand, whether writing about war in Nicaragua or the history and cultural salons of France.
